images.caption: Figure 3:Construction Procedure of HM-World. We combine (a) 3D scenes, (b) subjects, (c) subject trajectories, and (d) camera trajectories to render data containing dynamics in Unreal Engine 5.

Figure 5:Overview of HyDRA. (a) Memory Tokenization Module. (b) Dynamic retrieval attention computes relevance between the target query and memory tokens to retrieve the top-k relevant tokens, enabling the model to recall associated hybrid memory.
Figure 6:Qualitative comparison with other methods. The green boxes in the figure represent consistently generated subjects, while the red boxes stand for failure cases.

Figure 9:Qualitative comparison between retrieval methods. The upper displays frames selected by different methods, while the lower shows the generation results. Selected frames are the source frames of the selected tokens.

Figure 10:Distribution comparison of different retrieval methods. The x-axis and y-axis represent the token index and DiT layers, respectively. The bubble size and color reflect the selection frequency of each token during the entire denoising process. (a) The FOV overlap method yields a fixed token selection. (b) Our dynamic affinity method exhibits a diverse retrieval distribution, enabling the perception of richer memory contexts.
Figure 11:Qualitative comparison between different kernel sizes of the Memory Tokenizer. The red bounding boxes annotate the inconsistent region.
Figure 12:Qualitative comparison between the number of retrieved tokens. The red bounding boxes annotate the inconsistent region.

header: Appendix 0.CAdditional Examples from the HM-World Dataset
